pattern: With at most a handful of gold cells, an exhaustive depth-first walk that marks cells visited and backtracks explores every connected pickup path and keeps the richest total.
hints: You may start anywhere with gold, so every gold cell is a candidate starting point.
From a cell, recurse into its four neighbors that hold gold and are not already on the current path.
Backtrack: temporarily zero out the current cell so it cannot be revisited, sum the best of the four neighbor walks, then restore the cell before returning.

edge_inputs.description: central plus-shape forces a three-cell path
zeros split the grid so the path must snake around them
a full row of gold should all be collected
isolated gold cells, pick the single richest

stress.description: small grids with a limited number of gold cells so backtracking stays fast
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randrange(1, size + 1)
    n = rng.randrange(1, size + 1)
    grid = [[0] * n for _ in range(m)]
    cells = [(i, j) for i in range(m) for j in range(n)]
    rng.shuffle(cells)
    k = min(len(cells), rng.randrange(0, 12))
    for i, j in cells[:k]:
        grid[i][j] = rng.randrange(1, 101)
    return [grid]
stress.note: at most a dozen gold cells keeps the exponential search bounded

complexity.time: O(4^c) where c is the number of gold cells
